patch.value: MCP stdio server processes JSON-RPC lines concurrently → pipelined mutations on the same item lock-conflict

Found during pm-at83 dogfooding (manual MCP test). startMcpServer's readline 'line' handler runs each request fire-and-forget (`void (async () => {...})()` in src/mcp/server.ts), so requests are handled CONCURRENTLY rather than in arrival order. A client that pipelines two mutations on the same item without waiting for the first response gets a lock error on the second: e.g. sending pm_notes(add) + pm_learnings(add) for the same id in one stream returns id1=ok, id2=`Item pm-XXXX is locked (owner ...) (code 4)`. Well-behaved request/response stdio clients (Claude, Codex) serialize and do not hit this, so it is low severity and NOT a regression (the new narrow tools merely surface existing actions). Hardening options: (1) serialize tools/call handling via a simple async queue so requests are processed in arrival order; or (2) keep concurrency but document it and improve the lock-conflict message at the MCP boundary to hint 'await each response before sending the next'. Acceptance: a test pipelines two same-item mutations and both succeed (or the surfaced error explicitly explains serialization), existing MCP tests pass. Repro lives in the pm-at83 session notes.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: IMPLEMENTED (option 1: serialize stdio handling). Added src/core/shared/serial-queue.ts — a generic FIFO async queue (createSerialQueue: enqueue<T> returns the task's promise; internal tail is error-isolated so a rejecting task never wedges the chain; idle() awaits drain). startMcpServer now wraps each readline line in queue.enqueue(() => processRpcLine(line)) instead of fire-and-forget `void (async()=>{})()`, so JSON-RPC lines are processed in strict ARRIVAL ORDER. Extracted the per-line body into exported processRpcLine(line) for testability. RESULT: a client that pipelines two mutations on the same item (e.g. pm_notes add + pm_learnings add) no longer lock-conflicts on the second — the first releases its lock before the second begins. Coverage: serial-queue.ts added to vitest.config.ts include; unit tests merged into tests/unit/shared-helpers.spec.ts (arrival-order, result passthrough, reject-isolation, idle drain) to respect the 120-file tests/unit cap; integration test added to tests/integration/mcp-handshake.spec.ts proving pipelined same-item pm_notes+pm_learnings both succeed and both land (count=1 each). Targeted run: 2 files / 22 tests pass. server.ts itself stays out of the coverage allowlist (entry-point pattern) with the reusable primitive 100%-covered in core/shared.

value.author: maintainer-agent

Status: code is in working tree only, not committed/pushed per run constraint.
